methodsAttempted.notes: Search found official course syllabi and official news about AI curriculum expansion. The CS114 syllabus was used for a narrow course-content claim; broad news/promotional pages were not used as binding policy evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery did not identify a public university-wide SUSTech generative-AI use policy. Staged claims are therefore scoped to three official SUSTech-controlled sources: a Control Science and Engineering doctoral degree standard that addresses AI tools in thesis writing, a CS114 course specification for Introduction to Generative Artificial Intelligence, and a SUSTech Library AI-literacy embedded-teaching page. Official news and lecture pages were rejected for policy use where they were promotional or event-only.

stopConditions: Stop after verified SUSTech-controlled sources support direct AI-related claim candidates.
Do not infer university-wide binding policy from course specifications, library teaching reports, or news pages.
Reject official event, lecture, news, and promotional pages when they do not contain primary policy or guidance text.
Do not stage raw PDFs, HTML, screenshots, browser profiles, or full normalized text.

artifacts.claimText: SUSTech's Control Science and Engineering doctoral degree standard says that if AI tools such as language models or text-generation tools are used during thesis writing, their role must be auxiliary and they may not replace the degree applicant's core research work or independent thinking.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: 4月3日，在嵌入式教学第二讲“AI在学术研究中的边界与伦理”中，学科馆员张依兮介绍了AI幻觉和算法偏见、AI目前在学术研究场景下的利用，以及图书馆订购的学术AI工具。课程结合大量案例带同学们了解AI学术不端与伦理问题，并详细介绍了AI学术写作规范与引用标注指南，明确AI学术应用的“红线”与“绿道”，筑牢科研诚信的底线。

artifacts.title: Southern University of Science and Technology (SUSTech) staged AI policy evidence
artifacts.summary: Staged SUSTech evidence uses three official SUSTech-controlled sources. The strongest policy-specific evidence is a Control Science and Engineering doctoral degree standard that states AI tools used in thesis writing must be auxiliary, must not replace core research work or independent thinking, and should be supervised and confirmed in the originality statement. Additional teaching evidence comes from the official CS114 course specification and a SUSTech Library AI-literacy embedded-teaching page. No public university-wide SUSTech generative-AI use policy was identified in this crawl.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

The doctoral-thesis AI-tool claims are scoped to the Control Science and Engineering doctoral degree standard and should not be generalized to all SUSTech programs without additional official sources.
Course and library teaching claims describe educational content and guidance activities; they are not evidence of a binding university-wide AI policy.
Official AI news and lecture pages were rejected for policy claims because they were promotional or event-only sources.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
